provenance.title: Quantum Kernels and the Cross-Section of Stock Returns: Anatomy of a Vanishing Advantage
provenance.abstract: Do quantum kernels improve cross-sectional stock return prediction? A controlled horse race on Chinese A-shares shares identical training subsamples, solver, and tuning budgets across a quantum fidelity kernel, a projected quantum kernel, and a classical RBF control, so only the kernel is exchanged. On a point-in-time universe over 170 walk-forward windows (2012-2025), no quantum advantage exists after budget-equalization and family-wise correction. A 60-window evaluation on a survivorship-screened universe reproduces the opposite (spurious) conclusion, which the paper decomposes. Interaction characteristics help nothing; a widened bandwidth grid reveals an interior optimum; and the geometric difference does not predict out-of-sample gains.
key_claims: On a point-in-time universe with 170 walk-forward windows (2012-2025), the quantum fidelity kernel is statistically indistinguishable from a budget-matched classical RBF kernel (delta-IC=+0.005, p=0.42), and no pairwise difference among eleven models survives family-wise (Holm) correction.

A budget-equalized 2x2 design (kernel type x training size, with a Nystrom extension of the quantum kernel to the full ~37,800-observation budget) shows quantum kernels matching but never beating equal-budget linear ridge regression at either training scale.

A 60-window evaluation on a survivorship/full-sample-screened static universe reverses the conclusion: the same fidelity quantum kernel appears dominant on stability metrics (highest ICIR, t-stat, hit rate, smallest drawdown) and significantly beats neural baselines -- an artifact traced to universe construction, insufficient power/multiplicity, and weak deep-learning baselines.

Twelve literature-catalogued pairwise interaction characteristics do not improve any model (linear, boosted trees, or quantum), and actively hurt the quantum kernel's rotation pool (delta-IC=-0.031, p=0.03) by displacing stronger primitive signals from scarce qubits.

A widened 8-point bandwidth grid finds the tuned optimum at an interior bandwidth (not the near-classical endpoint a coarse grid suggests), and the regularized geometric difference g (Huang et al. 2021), though always >>1, is uncorrelated with realized out-of-sample gains (rho=-0.20).

architecture.primary_variant: Quantum Kernel Ridge Regression (QKRR) with fidelity kernel, under kernel-swap control vs. projected quantum kernel and classical RBF kernel

modules.name: CharacteristicConstructionAndStandardization
modules.input_shape: raw exchange snapshots + quarterly financial statements, daily, Jan 2010 - Mar 2026

parameters.standardization: cross-sectional, daily, over full market before universe restriction
parameters.fundamental_alignment: announcement-date merge; TTM aggregation only if 4 quarters span <=380 days; YoY growth requires 330-400 day span; forward-fill <=250 trading days

modules.notes: 27 characteristics reconstructible from raw data since 2010 (main study); +4 characteristics (RSRS, analyst revisions, variants) available only from 2020, for 31 total (diagnostic/short-sample study, Sec 6).

modules.name: UniverseConstruction_PointInTime
modules.output_shape: top-450 stocks by trailing 20-day mean float market cap, 2011-2025 (1.66M pool stock-days)
modules.notes: No information beyond day t enters -- constructible in real time. This is the 'clean' universe used for the main 170-window evaluation.

modules.name: UniverseConstruction_StaticScreen
modules.output_shape: fixed pool of 450 stocks with highest full-sample (2020-2025) price coverage
modules.notes: Deliberately unimplementable in real time (conditions on survival through the sample's end); used as the 'treatment arm' of the paper's natural experiment in research design (Sec 6).

modules.name: FactorRotationAndTop8Selection
modules.output_shape: top-8 characteristics by |window rank IC|, sign-corrected

parameters.training_window: trailing 252 trading days ending 20 days before rebalance date

modules.notes: The top-8 set is shared verbatim by all top-8 models (classical and quantum) -- this is the paper's main kernel-swap control input. The full active set (variable size >=6) is used only for classical full-set benchmarks (ridge, XGBoost full-set).

modules.name: BandwidthScaling

parameters.tuning_criterion: validation rank IC (80/20 temporal split of the kernel subsample)

modules.notes: Bandwidth governs spectral decay/exponential concentration (Shaydulin & Wild 2022; Canatar et al. 2023; Thanasilp et al. 2024).

modules.name: QuantumFeatureMapCircuit
modules.output_shape: quantum state |psi(x)> in a 2^8=256-dimensional complex Hilbert space

parameters.simulation: exact statevector simulation via PennyLane (Bergholm et al. 2018), batched -- no hardware

modules.notes: AMBIGUITY (see ambiguities[]): the single-qubit term exp(-i*x~_q*Z_q*H_q) uses H_q without explicit definition. Interpreted here as the standard IQP-style Hadamard-conjugated rotation (H . RZ(x~_q) . H = RX(x~_q) up to phase), matching Havlicek et al. (2019)-style encodings, but the paper does not spell this out.

modules.name: FidelityKernelComputation
modules.notes: Gram matrix follows from one inner-product matrix multiplication over all training states (paper's phrasing) -- i.e. batched overlap computation, not per-pair circuit re-execution.

modules.name: ProjectedKernelComputation
modules.output_shape: Bloch feature vector phi(x) in R^24, then scalar kernel value
modules.notes: gamma is a kernel bandwidth hyperparameter; the paper does not explicitly state its tuning grid/procedure (flagged in ambiguities[]).

modules.name: ClassicalRBFKernelControl

parameters.kernel: standard RBF on the same top-8 inputs used by the quantum kernels

modules.notes: This is the paper's kernel-swap control: identical inputs/subsample/solver/budget to the quantum kernels, isolating kernel geometry as the only varying factor.

modules.name: KernelRidgeRegressionSolver
modules.notes: Applied identically across the fidelity/projected/RBF kernel-swap triplet. Subsampling required because exact KRR is O(N^3).

modules.name: NystromExtension
modules.input_shape: Gram matrix computed against the 1,536-observation subsample as landmarks, extended to ~37,800 window observations

parameters.bandwidth: reuses the window's already-selected value from the subsample tuning step

modules.notes: AMBIGUITY: the paper cites Williams & Seeger (2001) for the Nystrom method but does not give the extension's explicit linear-algebra formula in this paper -- implementation must reconstruct the standard Nystrom KRR approximation from the cited reference.

modules.name: ClassicalBenchmarkSuite
modules.input_shape: top-8 set or full active set, ~37,800 full-window observations

parameters.nn3: 3 hidden layers 32-16-8, three-seed ensemble (Gu et al. 2020 / Leippold et al. 2022 architecture)
parameters.poly2ridge: ridge on all pairwise products of top-8 characteristics (Kozak et al. 2020 style)
parameters.ridge_int: ridge on active set + catalog interactions passing IC screen
parameters.qkrr_x: fidelity QKRR whose top-8 rotation pool includes catalog interactions

modules.notes: Eleven models total appear in the main evaluation's pairwise-comparison family (Sec 5.2).

connections.tensor_name: top-8 or full active-set characteristics, full ~37,800-obs window

differences.shared: identical N=1536 subsample, solver, alpha/bandwidth search budget -- only the kernel differs

differences.purpose: isolates kernel-type effect from training-budget effect
tests whether literature-catalogued pairwise interactions (Table 2) help any model class

mathematical_spec.name: Quantum feature-map encoding circuit
mathematical_spec.latex: |\psi(x)\rangle = \prod_{r=1}^{R}\left[\prod_{q=1}^{n} e^{-i\tilde x_q \tilde x_{q+1} Z_q Z_{q+1}} \prod_{q=1}^{n} e^{-i\tilde x_q Z_q H_q}\right]|0\rangle^{\otimes n}
undefined_symbols: H_q -- not explicitly defined; interpreted as a Hadamard-conjugated single-qubit rotation (see architecture.QuantumFeatureMapCircuit.notes and ambiguities[])

mathematical_spec.name: Projected quantum kernel (Huang et al. 2021)
hyperparameters: gamma (bandwidth of the projected-kernel RBF; tuning procedure not explicitly stated)

mathematical_spec.name: Regularized geometric difference (Huang et al. 2021)
hyperparameters: computed on a 400-point subset per window

optimizer.name: closed-form linear solve (no gradient descent) for all kernel/ridge models; standard gradient-based training for MLP/NN3/XGBoost benchmarks (optimizer unspecified for those)

lr_schedule.type: not applicable to KRR (closed-form); not specified for MLP/NN3 benchmarks

training_pipeline.batch_size: N=1536 stratified-by-date subsample (kernel models, per window); ~37,800 full window observations (classical full-budget models)
training_pipeline.curriculum: walk-forward re-fitting every 20 trading days (170 windows main study 2012-2025; 60 windows diagnostic study 2021-2025) -- not a curriculum in the ML sense but a rolling re-estimation protocol

datasets.name: Point-in-time universe (main study)
datasets.split: 170 out-of-sample walk-forward windows, rebalanced every 20 trading days, 2012-2025

reported_results.notes: nominal p=0.043 paired t, p=0.027 Wilcoxon -- confounded by budget, see 2x2 design
fidelity QKRR vs ridge -- nothing survives correction
Hybrid (QKRR+ridge), best model in diagnostic study
QKRR fidelity, highest ICIR in 16-model field
QKRR fidelity, smallest (best) drawdown in field

reported_results.metric: mean IC after point-in-time universe rematch
reported_results.notes: QKRR fidelity IC falls from 0.0512 to 0.0184 when matched to point-in-time universe

reported_results.metric: mean IC after point-in-time universe rematch
reported_results.notes: Ridge IC falls from 0.0567 to 0.0330 under the same rematch

reported_results.metric: difference-in-differences estimate (quantum excess sensitivity to universe)
reported_results.notes: p=0.71, bootstrap 95% CI [-0.039, +0.058] -- not statistically distinguishable

reported_results.metric: delta_IC (ridge with catalog interactions vs without)

reported_results.notes: nominal p=0.032; does not survive Holm correction

reported_results.metric: correlation (g vs realized quantum-minus-RBF IC differential)
reported_results.notes: geometric difference does not predict out-of-sample gains

reported_results.metric: mean validation IC by bandwidth (widened grid)

evaluation_protocol.compute: Exact statevector simulation via PennyLane (batched, Bergholm et al. 2018), n=8 qubits (256-dim state, classically tractable) -- explicitly no quantum hardware used; hardware/CPU/GPU specs for classical benchmarks and simulation runtime not stated.
special_conditions: Kernel-swap control: fidelity, projected, and RBF kernels share identical N=1536 stratified subsample, ridge solver, and alpha/bandwidth search grid per window -- isolates kernel geometry as the only varying factor.
Budget-equalized 2x2 design (kernel type x training size) to disentangle kernel geometry effects from O(N^3) sample-size constraints.
Holm family-wise correction applied to both the 10-pair main-study family and the diagnostic study's 8-pair family.
Portfolio evaluation: long-only top-30% equal-weighted, rebalanced every 20 days, 23 bp one-way transaction cost.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: The H_q term in the encoding circuit (Eq. 1) is interpreted as a Hadamard-conjugated RZ rotation (i.e. effectively an RX(x~_q) rotation up to global phase), the standard IQP-style single-qubit encoding term, since the paper gives no explicit gate-level definition of H_q.
implementation_assumptions.basis: standard convention in IQP-style quantum feature maps (Havlicek et al. 2019)
alternatives: H_q could denote a literal tensor-product operator Z_q (x) H_q acting jointly, which would be an unusual and non-standard construction
H_q could be a per-qubit Hadamard gate applied as a separate circuit layer rather than fused into the exponent

implementation_assumptions.assumption: The Nystrom extension (qkrr-nys) is implemented via the standard Nystrom KRR approximation (Williams & Seeger 2001): landmark-based low-rank Gram matrix approximation using the 1,536-observation subsample as landmarks, since the paper cites the method but does not restate its formula.
alternatives: a different Nystrom variant (e.g. with different regularization or landmark-selection scheme) could have been used

implementation_assumptions.assumption: The projected quantum kernel's bandwidth gamma is tuned per window on the same grid/procedure as the other hyperparameters (validation rank IC), since the paper states lambda's grid explicitly but not gamma's.
implementation_assumptions.basis: inferred by analogy with the paper's stated tuning procedure for lambda and alpha
alternatives: gamma could be fixed a priori (e.g. median heuristic) rather than tuned per window

implementation_assumptions.assumption: 'Stratified by date' subsampling to N=1536 draws a roughly proportional number of observations from each date within the 252-day training window, rather than e.g. stratifying by some other variable (sector, market cap).
implementation_assumptions.basis: most natural reading of 'stratified by date' given the walk-forward, daily-panel setting
alternatives: stratification could additionally condition on sector or size bucket, not stated

implementation_assumptions.assumption: MLP and NN3 benchmark models are trained with standard gradient-based optimization (e.g. Adam) since the paper specifies architecture (64-32; 32-16-8 ensemble) but not the optimizer, learning rate, or training-epoch budget.
implementation_assumptions.basis: common practice for the cited baseline architectures (Gu et al. 2020)
alternatives: the original Gu et al. (2020) / Leippold et al. (2022) training recipes could differ in optimizer/schedule specifics

ambiguities.description: The single-qubit encoding term exp(-i*x~_q*Z_q*H_q) includes an H_q operator that is never defined in the paper. The most likely reading (standard IQP-style construction) is that this represents a Hadamard-conjugated Z-rotation, i.e. an effective X-basis rotation, but the paper's literal notation (Z_q and H_q as if both are simultaneous operators in the same exponent) is not standard circuit notation and could be read multiple ways.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: H_q denotes Hadamard conjugation of the RZ(x~_q) gate (effectively RX(x~_q))
alternatives: a literal joint Z(x)H operator (non-standard, physically odd)
H_q as a separate Hadamard layer applied before/after the RZ, not fused into one exponential

ambiguities.description: The projected quantum kernel's bandwidth parameter gamma is introduced but its tuning grid/procedure is never stated, unlike lambda (explicit grids given in Sec 4.2 and Sec 8) and alpha (explicit grid in Sec 4.3).
ambiguities.primary_assumption: gamma is tuned per window via the same validation-rank-IC procedure as lambda and alpha
alternatives: gamma fixed via a median-heuristic or other closed-form rule, not cross-validated

ambiguities.description: The Nystrom extension formula used to scale the fidelity QKRR to the full ~37,800-observation budget is not given explicitly in this paper; only the cited method (Williams & Seeger 2001) and the landmark set (the 1,536-observation subsample) are specified.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: standard Nystrom KRR approximation as in the cited reference
alternatives: a nonstandard or modified Nystrom variant specific to this paper's implementation, not disclosed

ambiguities.description: 'Stratified by date' subsampling procedure (to draw N=1536 from a ~37,800-observation window) is named but its exact allocation scheme (proportional per date? fixed count per date? additional strata beyond date?) is not detailed.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: roughly proportional-to-date-size random sampling stratified only by date
alternatives: additional stratification by sector, size, or characteristic value, not stated
